response.justification: Bogdan brings over 16 years of broad software development experience, genuine depth in developer tooling, CI/CD infrastructure, and test frameworks, and visible passion for knowledge-sharing through workshops and talks. However, the role is explicitly a Level II people-management position requiring demonstrated experience managing engineers, setting performance expectations, and conducting career-development conversations — none of which appears anywhere in the resume. Every role listed is an individual-contributor position, even at the Staff level. Without evidence of people management, the candidate does not meet the stated minimum qualifications for this role, regardless of technical strength.
strengths: 16+ years of hands-on software development across web, mobile, embedded, and tooling domains exceeds the 8-year minimum by a wide margin

Direct developer-tooling and CI/CD experience (GitLab administration, CI servers, Docker/Artifactory, MCP code generators, test runner 'Trial', Fluent-Asserts library) aligns with the role's engineering-productivity focus
Demonstrated commitment to engineering culture via organized Refactoring Dojos, Code Katas, public workshops, and conference talks shows mentorship instinct relevant to team development
concerns: No people-management experience is evidenced anywhere — no direct reports, no performance reviews, no hiring, no org-chart ownership — which is a hard requirement for a Level II Manager role
No indication of strategic roadmap ownership, cross-team alignment, or leading organizational change at any scale, all of which are central to the responsibilities described
Mobile-platform depth is thin and dated (brief iOS/Apple TV work at WatchUp in 2015–2016) relative to the role's emphasis on a 'major mobile platform ecosystem'

key_factors.factor: No organizational leadership or roadmap ownership
